title: Pure Storage Array Data Reduction Ratio Collapse
dataSources: FlashArray `/array/space` or Unified Add-on array capacity events (JSON) indexed as `purestorage:*` or legacy `PureStorage_REST`
app: Pure Storage Unified Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5513)
spl:
index=storage (sourcetype="purestorage:*" OR sourcetype="PureStorage_REST") earliest=-7d
| eval drr=coalesce(data_reduction_ratio, space_data_reduction_ratio, total_reduction)
| where isnotnull(drr) AND drr > 0
| sort 0 _time
| stats first(drr) as drr_start last(drr) as drr_end by array_name
| eval drop_pct=round((drr_start-drr_end)/drr_start*100,1)
| where drr_start > 2 AND drop_pct > 25
| table array_name drr_start drr_end drop_pct
description: A sudden drop in data-reduction ratio often means new noisy workloads, encryption of source data, or loss of thin-provisioned savings—and usable physical capacity burns faster than forecasts.
value: Gives capacity owners early warning before free space cliffs, so they can rebalance volumes, change backup patterns, or expand arrays without emergency outages.
implementation: Ingest array-level space metrics at least hourly. Baseline the 7-day average reduction ratio per array and alert when the live ratio falls more than ~25% below that baseline. Validate field names against a sample event from your TA version; map REST `space.data_reduction` into `data_reduction_ratio` at ingest if the TA does not already.
visualization: Line chart (ratio trend), bar chart (delta vs baseline), table (array, drop %).

references.title: Pure Storage Unified Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase)
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Pure Storage

knownFalsePositives: Short spikes during approved changes, maintenance windows, or known batch jobs can match the rule; confirm against the vendor console and change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full your storage is getting and give you time to add space or clean up snapshots and old data before an application or job suddenly stops working.
